FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class ScrollAnimator

Namespace
FluentGpu.Animation
Assembly
FluentGpu.Animation.dll

Smooth scrolling (phase 7): eases each armed viewport's live offset toward its target (the WinUI inertial wheel feel), applies the content's -offset transform, re-realizes the virtual window at guard-band edges, and drives the auto-hiding scrollbar's WinUI "conscious" state machine. Only viewports actively scrolling/transitioning are ticked; zero work / zero alloc once everything settles (HasActive == false).

Conscious-scrollbar timing — verified against microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\CommonStyles\ScrollBar_themeresources.xaml:

  • Expand: pointer dwells over the scrollbar lane for ScrollBarExpandBeginTime = 400ms (:188), then the thumb grows / track+buttons fade in over ScrollBarExpandDuration = 167ms (:173) with KeySpline 0,0,0,1 (:587) — FluentPopOpen.
  • Contract (lane left, pointer still inside the viewport): begins after ScrollBarContractBeginTime = 500ms (:189), plays over ScrollBarContractDuration = 167ms (:176), same spline (:543).
  • Indicator fades over ScrollBarOpacityChangeDuration = 83ms (:174), linear (the storyboards' plain DoubleAnimation).
  • Idle auto-hide after a SCROLL: ScrollBarContractDelay = 2s (:178; ScrollViewer's SeparatorContractDelay is the same 2s — ScrollViewer_themeresources.xaml) then the 83ms fade.
  • ENGINE-DELIBERATE (documented deviation): when the pointer leaves the whole viewport and the bar was revealed by hover alone (no scroll since it appeared), it contracts immediately and retires after ContractBeginTime + ContractDuration (≈667ms) + the 83ms fade — WinUI would hold the expanded bar 500ms and the thin indicator the full 2s; an untouched hover-flash retiring early reads cleaner and keeps the frame loop idle. Scroll-revealed bars keep the WinUI 2s ContractDelay.
  • While the pointer rests inside the viewport the thin indicator stays visible (the WinUI MouseIndicator hold); it never idle-hides under the pointer.
public sealed class ScrollAnimator

ActiveCount

Viewports currently scrolling/transitioning (the armed set) — O(1) census.

ConsciousStateCount

Viewports retaining a conscious-scrollbar timer row (survives drops until fully hidden) — O(1) census.

Methods

Arm(NodeHandle) 

public void Arm(NodeHandle n)

Parameters

n NodeHandle

Hover(NodeHandle, bool)

Reveal a viewport's scrollbar (pointer is over the scrollable area) and reset its idle timer so it stays up while hovered; lane hover arms the WinUI 400ms-delayed expand.

public void Hover(NodeHandle n, bool overScrollbar)

Parameters

n NodeHandle
overScrollbar bool

public void Leave(NodeHandle n)

n NodeHandle

Tick(float)

dtMs float
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
